title: n8n loses connection with HTTP/3 enabled
symptom: When activating HTTP/3 on Traefik, n8n's editor shows 'no connection' when entering a workflow. There are no error messages in the console or Traefik logs.
applies_when: Using n8n behind Traefik Reverse Proxy
root_cause: n8n's editor relies on WebSockets for real-time communication, but HTTP/3 (QUIC) does not support WebSockets. When HTTP/3 is enabled, the browser attempts to negotiate H3, causing the WebSocket upgrade to fail, resulting in a 'no connection' message.
fix_steps: Open the Traefik configuration file for the n8n router.
Add the following TLS option to disable HTTP/3: 'no-h3' under 'alpnProtocols': ['h2', 'http/1.1'].
Save the configuration and restart Traefik.
agent_instruction: Disable HTTP/3 for the n8n router in Traefik's configuration.
